boundaries.title_guess: Germany & Italy: Ulm, Bavaria, Verona opera, Lake Orta, Monte Rosa, Val d'Aosta, Mont Blanc, Cluny, Montbard (summer 2012)

evidence.excerpt: at Ulm. Jewish window... We drove on through lovely grassy fields of Bavaria to Ellessee an old campsite on a lake.
Camping Castel San Pietro ... Verona Opera

boundaries.notes: Window opens mid-trip: page B_0193_L begins in the middle of a narrative (already at Ulm). This is the Verona-opera / Lake Orta / Monte Rosa Italy trip (book4 index entries 14-26). It continues from the previous window. Index page numbers do not match notebook 'no' fields here (notebook renumbered).

evidence.excerpt: A super trip. [then] August 30th Friday. Bourton Westwood Farm — 8.30. Simon, Eve & Tinder set off for a farm campsite South west of Much Wenlock.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Top of B_0208_R ends the Italy trip ('Our train was supposed to be 12:06 ... A super trip.'); the break falls at 'August 30th Friday. Bourton Westwood Farm', where the Much Wenlock trip begins.
boundaries.notes: Matches index entry 28 'St. Tropey, Boulogne, Aston Rowant / Much Wenlock' (St Tropez/Boulogne/Aston Rowant close the Italy trip; Much Wenlock opens this one).

boundaries.title_guess: Devizes & Foxhanger (Kennet & Avon Canal) with Charles, Jenny & John

evidence.excerpt: Friday September 28th — Simon, Eve & Tinder left at 5 to go to Devizes to spend the w/end with Charles & Jenny ... We're at Foxhangar

boundaries.notes: Date jump from early Sept (Much Wenlock) to Sept 28th. Matches index entry 29 'Devizes, Boxhanger' (Boxhanger ~ Foxhanger). B_0210_R is a glued-in canal leaflet belonging to this trip.

boundaries.title_guess: Wales: Grawen, Cefn Coed, Penderyn (visiting John)

evidence.excerpt: Friday May 17th. Simon, Eve + Tinder set off at 4.30 for Wales to see John. We are staying at Grawen ... The Station Hotel, Cefn Coed ... The distillery, Penderyn

boundaries.notes: Large date jump (Sept to May; year advances, likely 2013). Matches index entry 31 'Cefn Coed Austed'. B_0211_L is the tail of the Devizes trip ('all left ... on the way home').

evidence.excerpt: Saturday May 25th Peter's birthday. 11 Simon, Eve & Tinder left to go to Ansted so that Annette can chat with her executors ... After Sunday lunch we head towards Richard Holroyd in Cambridge ... drove home.

boundaries.notes: Short weekend outing to Ansted (Annette's home). Begins mid-page B_0212_L after the Wales trip ends. Could be read as a family-visit rather than a touring trip, but it is a van outing with departure/return, so kind=trip with moderate confidence. No clear index match (Ansted appears at index 31 'Austed' and later).

boundaries.title_guess: South of England: Standen, Capel, Petworth, Churt, Ash Vale

evidence.excerpt: Saturday June 15th — 9.40. we leave for the south of England. We visit Standen, the Beale family home
We set off for Petworth ... looked at the house

boundaries.mixed_detail: B_0212_R top continues the Ansted trip ('us on Sunday ... drove home.'); the break falls at 'Saturday June 15th — 9.40. we leave for the south of England'.
boundaries.notes: Matches index entries 32 'Standen, Capel...' and 33 'Steyning, Petworth' and 35 'Churt, Guildford...'. Trip ends at B_0216_L 'had supper at the White Horse with Charles + Jenny + drove on home.'

boundaries.title_guess: Hay on Wye (Rodnors Farm), Black Mountain Gliding, Raglan Castle

evidence.excerpt: Friday 28th June 2.35. Simon, Eve, Peter, Nana, Conrad (3½ m) + Tinder get off for Hay on Wye. ... Rodnors Farm
Glad Mountain Gliding Club ... On to Raglan Castle. ... Then on to home.

boundaries.mixed_detail: B_0216_R top (the 'Tulips & Roses' Jekyll postcard caption and 'drove on home') closes the South-of-England trip; the break falls at 'Friday 28th June 2.35.' where the Hay on Wye trip begins.
boundaries.notes: Matches index entry 36 'Hay, Raglan, Arras Kambrai' (Hay & Raglan belong to this trip; Arras/Kambrai open the next). Trip ends at B_0217_L 'On to Raglan Castle. ... Then on to home.'

boundaries.title_guess: France/Burgundy: Arras, Cambrai, St Quentin, Montbard, Abbaye de Fontenay, Provins, Reims, Le Crotoy

evidence.excerpt: Tuesday [?7/6]th August — 10 we left home + dropped Tinder off at kennels. ... at the Channel Tunnel ... We camped next to a lake ... between [?Arras] + Cambrai.
raining during our trip on to St. Quentin ... Labyrinthe at St Quentin, Picardie Cathedrals
We packed up & drove to Abbey de Fontenay

boundaries.mixed_detail: B_0217_L: top continues/closes the Hay trip ('On to Raglan Castle. ... Then on to home.'); the break falls at 'Tuesday [?7/6]th August — 10 we left home + dropped Tinder off at kennels ... Channel Tunnel', where the France trip departs.
boundaries.notes: Matches index entries 36-42 (Arras Kambrai, St Quentin Montbard, Buffon/Bourgogne Canal, Abbaye de Fontenay, Gouaix Provins, Bourget Commun Abbeville, Novellet). This trip continues past the end of the window (B_0222_R ends mid-narrative at Le Crotoy/Novelles, a Friday).

open_at_end: France/Burgundy trip starting B_0217_L continues past this window (B_0222_R ends mid-narrative at Le Crotoy / Novelles; index entries 42-43 'Novellet' / 'Marais, Watlington' fall beyond the window)
notes: All transcribed spreads in this window are volume B, notebook 'book4' territory (B_0193-B_0222). Notebook page 'no' fields are inconsistent with index page_no (the book was renumbered), so matching is by place name + narrative. Years are inferred: the opening Italy trip is 2012 (Verona opera ticket dated 29/07/2012); subsequent shorter UK outings then run through autumn 2012 and into 2013 (May/June/August), inferred from month headings and seasonal progression with no explicit years on the pages.
